summary: The `doc_build_status_check` job is a gate that exits 1 whenever an upstream documentation build job reports a non-success, non-skipped status. The interpolated condition shows the upstream doc build job has status `"failure"`, causing the first clause of the guard to evaluate false and the job to call `exit 1`.
root_cause: An upstream documentation build job finished with status 'failure'. The status-check gate script received that status interpolated as the literal string "failure", which satisfies neither '== "success"' nor '== "skipped"', so the script falls through to `exit 1`.

evidence.reason: The upstream doc-build job status has been interpolated as the literal string 'failure'; neither equality check can be true, so the overall condition is false.
The else branch executes `exit 1` precisely when the upstream job did not succeed or was not skipped, confirming the job fails because the upstream build failed.
Confirms the script exited with code 1, consistent with the false guard condition caused by the upstream 'failure' status.
Identifies this as the documentation build workflow, confirming the upstream failure is in the doc-build pipeline.

suggested_fix: Investigate and fix the upstream documentation build job that returned status 'failure'. The status-check gate is working as intended — once the upstream doc build job passes, this gate will automatically pass too.
